summary: 64 critical issues found — immediate action required.

findings.title: Table "business_analytics" Allows UPDATE as Anonymous User
findings.description: Any unauthenticated visitor can perform UPDATE on the "business_analytics" table without ownership scoping. This indicates missing or overly permissive Row Level Security policies. RLS authorization passed — the request was only rejected at the schema/constraint layer, confirming the table is writable beyond the auth boundary.

findings.title: PostgREST Schema Endpoint Publicly Accessible
findings.description: The PostgREST OpenAPI root (/rest/v1/) returned a full list of database table definitions to an unauthenticated request. This exposes your entire data model without authentication.
findings.remediation: In Supabase project settings → API, set "Expose schemas" to empty or restrict the exposed schema list. Consider adding a JWT requirement to schema introspection via a custom policy.

findings.title: Sensitive Columns Exposed in "businesses" Table
findings.description: The "businesses" table is readable without authentication and contains columns matching sensitive data patterns: address, phone. PII columns must be scoped to the owning user only.

findings.title: RPC Function "get_my_competitor_ad_metrics" Accessible to Anonymous Users
findings.description: The RPC function "get_my_competitor_ad_metrics" is accessible to unauthenticated callers via POST /rest/v1/rpc/get_my_competitor_ad_metrics. Any visitor can invoke this function without a valid session.

findings.title: Sensitive Columns Exposed in "site_settings" Table
findings.description: The "site_settings" table is readable without authentication and contains columns matching sensitive data patterns: contact_email, support_phone, require_email_verification, email_provider, resend_api_key, sendgrid_api_key, mailjet_api_key, mailjet_api_secret, email_from, office_address, office_phone.

findings.title: Row Count Leaked for RLS-Blocked Table "businesses"
findings.description: Although RLS prevents reading data from "businesses", the total row count (4238) is leaked via the Content-Range header on a HEAD request. An attacker knows this table has 4238 rows even without being able to read them.

findings.title: Authenticated Scan Skipped
findings.description: LeakScope could not create a throwaway account. Reason: Signup failed: email rate limit exceeded

evidence.skipReason: Signup failed: email rate limit exceeded

findings.remediation: No action required. Unauthenticated checks still ran.
